name: ascii-canvas

metadata.name: ascii-canvas
metadata.summary: Treat strings like Items on a 2D Canvas.

documentation:
[![Version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/ascii_canvas.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/ascii_canvas/)

# Treat Strings like Items on a 2D Canvas

With this primitive library you can do things like this:

```python
from ascii_canvas import canvas
from ascii_canvas import item

canvas_ = canvas.Canvas()

rect_a = item.Item('+-----+\n|Hello|\n+-----+', position=[0, 0])
rect_b = item.Item('+-----+\n|World|\n+-----+', position=[16, 5])
rect_c = item.Item('+-+\n|!|\n+-+', position=[32, 0])
line_a = item.Line(start=[7, 1], end=[15, 6])
line_b = item.Line(start=[23, 6], end=[31, 1])

canvas_.add_item(rect_a)
canvas_.add_item(rect_b)
canvas_.add_item(rect_c)
canvas_.add_item(line_a)
canvas_.add_item(line_b)
print(canvas_.render())
```

Which results in this output:

```
+-----+                         +-+
|Hello|----+               +----|!|
+-----+    |               |    +-+
           |               |
           |               |
           |    +-----+    |
           +----|World|----+
                +-----+
```

modules.name: ascii_canvas.canvas
documentation: Aggregate items into a string canvas.

classes.name: ascii_canvas.canvas.Canvas

methods.name: ascii_canvas.canvas.Canvas.__init__
documentation: Hold a list of Items.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.canvas.Canvas.add_item
documentation:
Insert the item at the given index.

Args:
    item: The item to add
    index: The index or `-1` for append

methods.name: ascii_canvas.canvas.Canvas.bbox
documentation: Encompass all items in the bounding box.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.canvas.Canvas.render
documentation:
Fill it with placeholders and add the items to it.

Args:
    line_numbers: Whether to show line numbers
Returns:
    The rendered string

class_variables.name: ascii_canvas.canvas.Canvas.BLANK
documentation: The symbol to use for the `Canvas` background.
Aggregate string objects onto a 2D string canvas.

modules.name: ascii_canvas.item
documentation: Items can be placed on the Canvas.

classes.name: ascii_canvas.item.Item

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Item.__init__
documentation: Hold a text and a position.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Item.text
documentation: The text to display.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Item.position
documentation: The 2D position of the item.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Item.bbox
documentation: Make the bbox encompass the text.
Defined by an arbitrary text and a position on the Canvas.

classes.name: ascii_canvas.item.Line

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Line.__init__
documentation: Define the line by a start and an end point.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Line.bbox
documentation: Make the bbox encompass the entire line.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Line.text
documentation:
Create a string representing the line.

1. Go half way horizontally
2. Go straight down to the end of y
3. Continue rest horizontally

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Line.position
documentation: Offset the position if the start is left of the end.

A line between two points.

Example:

```
START---+
        |
        |
        +---END
```

classes.name: ascii_canvas.item.Rectangle

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Rectangle.__init__
documentation: Define the shape, position and render options for the Rectangle.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Rectangle.text
documentation: Width and height have to be at least 1 in order for it to render.

methods.name: ascii_canvas.item.Rectangle._horizontal
documentation: Create a horizontal line.

A rectangle.

Example:

```
+---+
|   |
|   |
+---+
```
